meaning: n.学徒期满的职工,熟练工人
examples: It would be indecent to compare either a curate or a chaplain with a journeyman in any common trade.
Instead, he immediately became an electrician after high school, working as an apprentice and eventually becoming a journeyman.
Pity the big man. Many assume that sprouting to a 7-foot height alone should guarantee a career as an NBA journeyman at the very least.
They picked up troubled journeyman lineman Albert Haynesworth and media-friendly wide receiver Chad Ochocinco.
She turns in a performance of which many a journeyman thesp would be proud.
